introduction: A sturdy chest reinforced with iron bands sits half-buried in rubble. The metal shows signs of age but remains solid - built to withstand the test of time.
This chest bears the mark of the old kingdom's quartermaster corps - a crossed sword and quill. Below it, a date: '1247, Third Age.' Over two centuries old.
A small plaque reads: 'Emergency provisions - authorized personnel only.' Well, you're about as authorized as anyone is going to get these days.
You work the ancient lock open. Inside, you find military-grade supplies remarkably well-preserved. The old quartermaster corps knew their business.
